title: 10 best herbal tinctures for frizzy hair

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its remarkable ability to improve circulation and stimulate hair growth. The antioxidants present in rosemary also help to repair damage caused by heat styling tools and environmental stressors, resulting in smoother, more manageable locks. Additionally, rosemary's antiseptic properties can help to reduce irritation and inflammation on the scalp, leading to a healthier and less frizzy mane. Regular use of rosemary tincture can leave hair looking sleek, shiny, and effortlessly styled.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds help protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can calm irritated hair strands and prevent frizz-causing breakage.

Stimulant properties: Rosemary's stimulant properties help increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy circulation that can improve hair growth and reduce frizz by strengthening hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Camphor: Helps to reduce frizz by creating a protective barrier on the hair surface, preventing moisture from entering and causing it to become frizzy.

Bornyl acetate: Acts as an antifungal and antiseptic agent, helping to control scalp infections and inflammation that can lead to frizzy hair, while also reducing protein loss and strengthening hair follicles.
Carnosic acid: Exhibits antioxidant properties, protecting the hair shaft from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness, brittleness, and frizz.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help calm and smooth frizzy hair.
Flowers: Used for their antiseptic and antifungal properties to help control scalp irritation and reduce frizz.
Stems: Used for their astringent and antiseptic properties to help balance scalp pH and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 parts 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Combine the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and vodka or glycerin in the jar and seal the lid tightly.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Label and store the rosmarinus officinalis tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it provides a natural way to smooth and tame unruly locks. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in stinging nettle help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and dryness that can lead to frizz. Additionally, the tincture's ability to strengthen hair follicles and improve blood circulation helps to promote healthy hair growth, leaving your locks looking smooth, shiny, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to frizz and flyaways by calming irritated skin.
Antioxidant properties: Nettle's antioxidants help to protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, leading to smoother, less-frizzy hair.

Hormone-balancing properties: Stinging nettle has been shown to balance hormones, including those that regulate sebum production, which can lead to frizz and unmanageable hair; balancing hormones can help regulate oil production and reduce frizz.
remedy_constituents: Stylopside: This alkaloid helps with frizzy hair by smoothing the cuticle layer, reducing the rough texture and flyaways associated with frizz.
Furanocoumarins: These phenolic compounds help to strengthen hair follicles and improve the overall structure of hair, leading to a reduction in frizz and an increase in hair's natural shine.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin helps to stabilize the hair's cuticle layer and protect it from environmental stressors, reducing the likelihood of frizz and flyaways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of urtic acid and other compounds that help to reduce frizz and add shine to hair.
Roots: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the hair and scalp.
Stems: Employed for their ability to provide essential nutrients and minerals that promote healthy hair growth and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems, taking care not to irritate skin or eyes.
Chop 1 part of harvested plant material into small pieces, using a sharp blade or scissors.
Combine chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking jar every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to nourish and smooth the hair shaft. The herbal extract penetrates deep into the hair, calming frizz-causing inflammation and sealing the cuticle layer to lock in moisture. As a result, frizzy hair becomes more manageable, with less volume and more shine. Additionally, aloe tinctures' natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce flyaways and add a soft, silky texture to the hair, leaving it looking healthy and radiant.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing property: Aloe vera's moisturizing property helps to lock in hydration and smooth out the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways by providing long-lasting nourishment.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in aloe vera tincture help soothe and calm irritated scalps and reduce inflammation that can cause frizzy hair.
Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness, brittleness, and frizz.
remedy_constituents: Amino Acids: Amino acids, particularly glycine, proline, and alanine, help to strengthen hair follicles, reduce frizz by improving hair elasticity, and promote a smoother hair texture.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly glucomannan and acemannan, help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz by creating a protective barrier around the hair shaft, and enhance the hair's natural shine.
Saponins: Saponins, particularly aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce frizz by creating a gentle, non-ionic surfactant that cleanses and conditions the hair, leaving it soft, smooth, and manageable.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to nourish and moisturize hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce inflammation, leading to healthier and smoother hair.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds are a good source of fatty acids and other nutrients that help to lock in moisture and protect the hair from damage, resulting in a smoother and more manageable texture.
remedy_recipe: Chop 100g of fresh aloe vera leaves into small pieces for easy infusion.
Combine the chopped aloe vera with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to balance and regulate the scalp's natural oil production. The herbal extract has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that soothe an itchy, irritated scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking. Additionally, lemongrass oil's light, citrusy scent helps to smooth and calm flyaways, leaving hair looking sleeker and more manageable. By promoting a healthy scalp environment, lemongrass tinctures can significantly reduce frizz and add shine to even the most unruly locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting healthy, shiny hair.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tincture can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce irritation, which can contribute to frizzy hair.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tincture's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help control fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can cause flaking, dryness, and frizz.
remedy_constituents: Geranial: This monoterpenoid alcohol has a calming effect on the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to frizzy hair. Its soothing properties may also help to smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways.

Citronellal: As a monoterpenoid aldehyde, citronellal has potent antioxidant properties that can help protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage, which can contribute to frizzy hair. Its antioxidant activity may also help to stabilize the hair's natural moisture balance.

Limonene: This terpene has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to control scalp infections and irritation that may lead to frizzy hair. Its ability to reduce fungal growth can also help to prevent dandruff and other scalp issues that can contribute to frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part for making tinctures is the Leaves: which are used to combat frizzy hair due to their high content of citral, an essential oil that helps to smooth and calm the hair.
Rhyzomes: The second most used part is the Rhyzomes: as they are a rich source of essential oils, which help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Buds: The third most used part is the Buds: which are used in tinctures to help control frizz and add manageability to hair due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems and dry them in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours at 35c.
Combine the dried plant material with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create a tincture.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Filter the tincture through a 0.2 micron filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that calm the scalp, reducing irritation and itchiness. This, in turn, allows the cuticles to lay flat, smoothing the hair shaft and minimizing frizz. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects help to reduce protein loss from the hair, leading to stronger, healthier locks that are less prone to frizz and more manageable overall. Regular use of chamomile tinctures can lead to a silky, smooth finish that's perfect for any hair type.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and frizz caused by dryness and irritation.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage hair cuticles, leading to frizz and unmanageability, while also protecting the hair from environmental stressors.
Soothing and calming properties: Chamomile's apigenin flavonoids help calm the nervous system, reducing tension and stress that can cause hair to become brittle and prone to frizz.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid constituent helps to reduce frizz by soothing the scalp, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress that can damage hair.

Matricaria ester: A terpenoid constituent, Matricaria ester has been shown to possess anti-frizz properties by smoothing the hair cuticle and reducing moisture loss, thereby reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
Luteolin: This flavonoid constituent has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the scalp, reduce frizz, and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high content of apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms the scalp.
Leaves: Used for their ability to balance the scalp's pH and reduce frizz by regulating the hair's natural oil production.
Seeds: Used for their richness in fatty acids and antioxidants that help to nourish and protect the hair from damage and frizz.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka.
Combine the flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains antioxidants that help to strengthen hair follicles, reducing damage caused by heat styling tools and environmental factors. The herb's antiseptic properties also soothe an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, thyme's natural astringent qualities help to balance the scalp's oil production, leaving hair looking smoother and more manageable. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting healthier-looking locks.

remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair by reducing the surface tension of water on the hair shaft, resulting in a sleeker appearance.
Thymol: Possesses antioxidant properties that protect the hair from environmental damage, reducing frizz and flyaways caused by free radicals.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the scalp and reduce irritation, contributing to a healthier hair follicle environment that leads to smoother, less frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: They are used because they contain thymol, a compound that helps to reduce frizz and add shine to hair.
Buds: They are used due to their high concentration of thymol and other compounds that help to protect and nourish the hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, and chop them finely with a knife.
Combine the chopped thymus vulgaris with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Transfer the thymus vulgaris tincture to small glass bottles and label them, ready to use within 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp. The herbal remedy works by reducing inflammation and irritation, which can cause frizz and flyaways. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, leaving strands looking smoother and more manageable. By using a pot marigold tincture as a hair treatment or adding it to your shampoo, you can achieve a sleeker, less-frizzy locks with regular use.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce scalp irritation and soothe itchiness caused by frizzy hair, allowing for smoother and more manageable locks.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marigold tincture, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and flyaways by strengthening the hair shaft and promoting healthy growth.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: Marigold tincture's emollient compounds, such as glycerides and saponins, help to lock in moisture, smooth the hair cuticle, and add shine to dry and frizzy hair, making it look healthier and more vibrant.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz by forming a protective barrier on the hair shaft, preventing moisture loss and damage.
Sesquiterpenes: Contribute to the reduction of frizz by reducing inflammation and soothing the scalp, which can help to calm and smooth the hair.

Phenolic acids (including flavonoids and phenolic acids): Have antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair from environmental stressors, such as heat and UV damage, which can contribute to frizz and breakage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and triterpenoids that help to condition and protect hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.
Leaves: They are used because they contain antioxidants and saponins that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.
Roots: They are used because they contain inulin and other compounds that help to nourish and strengthen the hair follicles, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair texture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in full bloom and dry them thoroughly to preserve their potency and aroma.
Measure 1 part of dried calendula flowers to 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the extraction to occur naturally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in silica, a natural mineral that strengthens and nourishes the hair shaft. Silica helps to smooth the cuticle of the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways by locking in moisture and adding shine. Additionally, field horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, further reducing frizz and promoting healthy, manageable locks. Regular use of field horsetail tincture can lead to silky, smooth hair that looks and feels great.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail tincture is rich in silica, a mineral that helps to strengthen and fortify hair follicles, leading to healthier and less frizzy locks.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in horsetail tincture help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and frizz.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm irritated scalps and hair follicles, reducing inflammation that can lead to frizzy and unmanageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Furanosesquiterpenes: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce irritation and inflammation in the scalp, thereby reducing frizz and promoting smoother hair.

Phenolics: Phenolics in Equisetum arvense have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the hair from environmental stressors, such as heat and humidity, that can cause frizz and damage to the hair.

Sesquiterpenoids: Some research suggests that sesquiterpenoids in Equisetum arvense may have a beneficial effect on hair texture, possibly by reducing the cuticle layer's thickness and improving hair's natural shine, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Stems: They are used because of their high silica content, which can help smooth and straighten hair.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide antioxidants that help protect hair from damage and frizz.
Roots: They are used for their potential to stimulate hair growth and reduce frizz by nourishing the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh equisetum arvense plant material by selecting the rhizomes and roots for tincture preparation.
Chop the gathered equisetum arvense plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the plant material in the ethanol mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's natural pH level. The herb's antiseptic properties soothe an itchy, irritated scalp, reducing dandruff and flakiness that can contribute to frizz. Additionally, wormwood's astringent properties help to tighten the cuticle layer of the hair shaft, smoothing out rough edges and reducing frizz-causing flyaways. By promoting a healthy scalp and hair environment, wormwood tinctures work to tame even the most unruly locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Wormwood's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation that can cause frizz and flyaways.
Astringent Properties: Wormwood's astringent properties help tighten the hair follicles and scalp, reducing excess oil production and frizz, while also helping to smooth and tame unruly hair.

Antimicrobial Properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial properties help combat dandruff, itchiness, and other scalp issues that can contribute to frizzy hair, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone, a terpene found in Artemisia absinthium, may help smooth hair cuticles and reduce frizz by its antiseptic and astringent properties.
Lupulin: Lupulin, a sesquiterpene lactone, may help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture, reducing frizz and enhancing manageability.
Absinthin: Absinthin, a phenolic compound, may help to protect hair from environmental stressors and damage, leading to a smoother and more frizz-free appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their astringent and antiseptic properties to help control oil production and reduce frizz.
Stems: Employed for their antioxidant and antifungal properties to protect the scalp and reduce inflammation that can lead to frizzy hair.
Buds: Utilized for their ability to help balance the scalp's pH and reduce excess oil production, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day.
Dry the plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or a drying screen in a warm dry place.
Combine 1 part dried artemisia absinthium with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its natural properties that calm and soothe the scalp. The herb's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities help to reduce irritation and dryness, which are common causes of frizz. Additionally, lavender's ability to regulate sebum production ensures that hair retains just the right amount of moisture, leading to a smoother, more manageable texture. By incorporating English lavender tinctures into your hair care routine, you can achieve a silky, sleek finish that's free from flyaways and frizz.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Lavender's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, which can damage hair cuticles and contribute to frizz, leaving your hair looking smoother and more manageable.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tincture can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation that can lead to frizzy hair, promoting a healthier scalp environment for your locks.

Antibacterial: Lavender's antibacterial properties can help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oil buildup and frizz, while also preventing infections that can damage hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, which may help calm frizzy hair by reducing irritation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has antioxidant properties, helping to protect the hair and scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to frizz.
Lavandulol: A terpene with a potential anti-inflammatory effect, which may help to reduce inflammation on the scalp and promote a balanced scalp environment, leading to reduced frizz.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to protect hair from damage and add shine.
Stems: Contains antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to purify and balance the scalp, reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated with scissors or pruning shears.
Clean the harvested flowers thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime tinctures helps with frizzy hair because its natural oils and antioxidants work to smooth and calm the cuticle layer, reducing frizz and adding shine. The acidic properties of lime help to balance the pH of the scalp and hair, which can become imbalanced due to environmental factors or product buildup, leading to frizz and unmanageability. Additionally, lime's astringent properties help to tighten the hair shaft, further reducing frizz and flyaways, leaving hair looking sleek and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lime juice contains antioxidants like vitamin C, which helps neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cuticles and cause frizz, resulting in smoother, more manageable locks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal ingredients in lime tinctures, such as aloe vera or chamomile, have anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated scalps and calm the hair follicles, reducing frizz and promoting healthier hair growth.

Humectant and emollient properties: Lime juice and other herbal extracts in the tincture work to lock in moisture, detangle, and smooth the hair shaft, leaving it with a soft, silky texture that's less prone to frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming frizzy hair.
Barks: High in flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to strengthen and smooth hair cuticles, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia europaea leaves and flowers fresh or dried in the morning dew for optimal potency.
Chop 20 grams of dried tilia europaea herb into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped herb with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing for at least two-thirds space between contents and lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking gently once daily during that period for thorough infusion.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its natural ability to balance the scalp's pH levels and reduce oil production. The tannins present in fennel help to tighten the cuticles on the hair shaft, resulting in a smoother, more manageable texture. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy scalp, reducing frizz-causing irritation. By using an herbal fennel tincture as a pre-shampoo treatment or a leave-in conditioner, you can say goodbye to frizzy locks and hello to silky, healthy-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting healthy, shiny hair.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, which helps to minimize frizz and add smoothness to the hair.
Astringent properties: Fennel's astringent properties help to tighten and clarify the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways by smoothing the hair shaft.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain omega-3 fatty acids that nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at dawn when the dew is still present.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris then air-dry it completely.
Combine 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare with 500 milliliters of vodka in a glass jar for a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and allow it to macerate for eight weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean amber glass bottle then discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its ability to balance the scalp's natural pH levels, reducing excessive oil production that can lead to frizz. The antioxidants present in elderberry also help to protect the hair shaft from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, elder tincture can help to strengthen hair follicles, leading to healthier, smoother locks with less frizz.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Elder tincture's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cuticles, leading to frizz and unmanageability.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tincture can soothe an itchy scalp, reduce redness, and calm inflammation that can cause frizzy hair.
Antifungal and Antibacterial Properties: Elder tincture's antifungal and antibacterial properties help combat dandruff, itchiness, and other scalp issues that can lead to frizz and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming frizzy hair.
Buds: Elder buds contain a high concentration of anthocyanins, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers in late summer when they are fully open and dry them immediately.
Combine one part dried sambucus nigra flowers with two parts vodka or high-proof spirits in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar weekly during this time.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in the fridge for up to two years, using 10-20 drops per application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's natural pH levels. The tannins present in hawthorn extract help to reduce excess oil production, while also calming irritation and soothing dryness. This results in a smoother, more manageable texture that is less prone to flyaways and frizz. Additionally, the ant-inflammatory properties of hawthorn tincture can help to repair damaged hair cuticles, leaving your locks looking healthy, shiny, and effortlessly stylish.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and flyaways by preventing oxidative stress on the hair shaft.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to dryness, brittleness, and frizziness of the hair, leading to healthier-looking locks.

Vasodilatory properties: Hawthorn's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of frizzy hair by increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to the hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are used in tinctures to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing dandruff and itchiness associated with frizzy hair.
Bark: The bark of the hawthorn plant is rich in flavonoids, which help to strengthen hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers measuring one part herb to three parts vodka.
Combine the herb mixture with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar covering the herbs completely.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the resulting tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains hyperforin, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and promotes healthy scalp circulation. By soothing irritated scalps and stimulating blood flow, JTW tinctures help to calm frizz-causing itchiness and flakiness, leaving hair looking smoother and more manageable. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to repair damage caused by styling tools and environmental stressors, resulting in a healthier, less frizzy mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and unruliness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can lead to frizzy, irritated hair. By reducing inflammation, St John's Wort tincture can help soothe and calm the scalp.
Antibacterial properties: St John's Wort has antibacterial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff, itchiness, and frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to calm frizz and add shine to hair.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe itchy scalp and reduce frizz-causing irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean dried st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum in amounts of 2 ounces per quart of vodka.
Combine the dried st john's wort flowers with vodka in a clean glass jar to create a ratio of one part herbs to four parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure all herb particles come into contact with the liquid.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles label and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its ability to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and minimizing frizz. The antiseptic properties of dandelion root also help to calm an itchy scalp, preventing irritation that can lead to frizzy hair. Additionally, the herb's ability to stimulate blood flow promotes healthy hair growth, giving you a smoother, more manageable mane. By using herbal dandelion tincture as part of your hair care routine, you can say goodbye to unruly locks and hello to silky, soft hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which can help reduce inflammation in the scalp and hair follicles, leading to smoother, less frizzy hair.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant properties can help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy, shiny hair that is less prone to frizz.

Detoxifying effects: Dandelion's ability to stimulate liver function and promote detoxification can help remove impurities and toxins from the scalp and hair, leading to healthier, more manageable hair that is less frizzy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming the scalp to reduce frizz.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and other essential nutrients, they help nourish and moisturize the hair follicles, reducing frizz and promoting smoothness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers taraxacum officinale, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Wash the dandelion flowers with pure water and gentle soap, then rinse thoroughly with clean water.
Combine 500g of the washed dandelion flowers with 1000ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the plant material.
Bottle the resulting tincture and store it in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years, using 10-15 drops per application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's pH level, reducing frizz-causing irritation. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties soothe dry and damaged hair follicles, allowing for healthier hair growth. Additionally, licorice tincture's natural humectant properties help retain moisture in the hair shaft, leaving it soft, smooth, and manageable. Regular use can also enhance hair's elasticity and reduce flyaways, resulting in a silky, frizz-free finish that looks and feels amazing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz caused by inflammation.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice root is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to frizz.
Moisturizing properties: Licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhetic acid, which can help lock in moisture and hydrate the hair, reducing dryness and frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to create tinctures that help to smooth and calm frizzy hair, thanks to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark of the licorice plant contains compounds that help to lock in moisture, reducing frizz and adding shine to dry and damaged hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried licorice root and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Pour 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka over the licorice root in the jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4 weeks allowing extraction.
After the extraction period strain the liquid from the jar through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the resulting tincture to amber glass bottles and label them with ingredients and date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato tinctures helps with frizzy hair because its unique blend of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals nourish and balance the scalp, reducing inflammation and repairing damaged hair follicles. The tincture's natural humectant properties also help to lock in moisture, smoothing the hair cuticle and leaving locks silky and manageable. As a result, frizz is reduced, and hair becomes stronger, healthier, and more resilient to humidity and styling stress.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Found in herbs like chamomile and calendula, saponins can help to create a gentle cleanser for the scalp and hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Antioxidants: Ingredients rich in antioxidants, such as green tea or ginkgo biloba, can help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness.
Humectant properties: Herbs like aloe vera or coconut oil contain natural humectants that can help lock in moisture and reduce frizz by attracting and retaining hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and starch, the roots help to calm and smooth frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and excess oil.
Seeds: Packed with omega-3 fatty acids and vitamins, the seeds nourish and moisturize dry, damaged hair, helping to tame frizz and add shine.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh taraxacum kok saghyz leaves and chop them into small pieces to ensure maximum surface area.
Combine the chopped herb with 1000 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, squeezing the cloth gently to extract as much tincture as possible.
Transfer the resulting tincture to amber glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, shaking well before use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for frizzy hair are natural remedies that help to tame and smooth unruly locks by controlling frizz and adding shine. These tinctures work by balancing the scalp's natural pH, reducing moisture loss, and providing antioxidants. The best herbal tinctures for frizzy hair include Rosmarinus officinalis, Urtica dioica, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Avena sativa, Thymus vulgaris, Equisetum arvense, and Cassia auriculata. If left untreated, frizzy hair can lead to social anxiety, low self-esteem, and a negative impact on daily life, making it essential to incorporate these tinctures into your hair care routine.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frizzy hair that are treated with herbal tinctures are dry skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. Herbal tinctures like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and flakiness. Additionally, herbal remedies may also be used to treat dandruff, acne, and rosacea, all of which share similarities with frizzy hair in terms of dryness, itchiness, or irritation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with frizzy hair is a blend of rosemary, lavender, and argan oil-infused tinctures. Rosemary tincture stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz. Lavender tincture calms the hair follicles, reducing inflammation and soothing dryness. Adding argan oil-infused tincture helps to nourish and protect the hair from environmental damage, leaving it smooth, silky, and manageable. Mix equal parts of each tincture and apply to the hair after shampooing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains high levels of antioxidants and essential oils that promote healthy hair growth and reduce frizz. The tannins and saponins present in the tincture help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture, making it smoother and more manageable. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Rosmarinus officinalis help to soothe an itchy scalp, reducing the appearance of frizz and flyaways. This results in a more polished and sleek hairstyle.
remedy_constituents: Camphor: Helps reduce frizz by creating a protective barrier on the hair shaft, smoothing the cuticle and preventing moisture from entering.
Bornel: Contributes to reducing frizz by improving the hair's elasticity and flexibility, allowing it to move freely without becoming brittle or prone to breakage.
Bornyl acetate: Helps to smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine, while also providing antioxidant properties to protect the hair from environmental damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used parts due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce frizz and add shine to hair.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis contain a significant amount of rosmarinic acid and other antioxidants, which help to protect hair from damage and reduce frizz.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are also used to make tinctures due to their high content of essential oils and antioxidants, which help to nourish and protect hair from damage and frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at their peak potency and store them in a clean environment.
Combine 250 grams of the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers with 750 milliliters of 45% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its natural properties that provide intense nourishment and hydration to the scalp and hair. The herb contains a rich source of antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, which help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the scalp and promote a healthy environment for hair growth. This results in smoother, shinier, and more manageable hair that looks and feels amazing. Regular use of Urtica dioica tinctures can lead to long-lasting frizz-free hair.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizz and breakage.
Saponins: Saponins have surfactant properties that help to clean and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and making it more manageable.
Polysaccharides: These compounds help to lock in moisture and protect the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and leaving the hair looking smoother and more healthy.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and vitamins that help to nourish and protect the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Roots: They contain compounds that help to strengthen and repair damaged hair, reducing frizz and improving overall hair health.
Stems: They are a good source of silica, which helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture, reducing frizz and adding smoothness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems on a sunny day when moisture is low.
Clean the harvested material with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the cleaned urtica dioica with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains soothing and moisturizing properties that calm the scalp and lock in moisture. The aloe vera gel helps to repair and restore the hair's natural moisture balance, reducing frizz and flyaways. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce irritation and promote a healthy scalp environment. As a result, frizzy hair becomes smoother, shinier, and more manageable, making it an effective natural remedy for achieving silky and healthy-looking locks. Regular use can lead to noticeable improvements.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps to reduce frizz by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme that breaks down the hair's natural moisture barrier, leading to a smoother and more manageable hair texture.
Aloe-emodin: Contributes to reduced frizz by exhibiting antioxidant properties that protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, preserving its natural shine and reducing flyaways.
Cinnamic acid: Helps to minimize frizz by enhancing the hair's natural elasticity and flexibility, allowing it to withstand humidity and heat styling without becoming unruly or unmanageable.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Leaves: Contain vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which help to nourish and moisturize dry, frizzy hair.
Leaves: Rich in glycoproteins and polysaccharides, which help to smooth and tame frizzy hair, reducing its unmanageability.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 mature aloe barbadensis leaves on a sunny day and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and mix 1 part of the chopped leaves with 5 parts of 80-proof vodka.
Combine the leaf-vodka mixture into a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and label it.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique combination of properties. The antifungal and antiseptic qualities of the herb help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil buildup and excess moisture that can lead to frizz. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of the tincture can help to relax the hair follicles, promoting a smoother cuticle layer and reducing frizz. This results in a more manageable and silky texture for your hair.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce frizz by its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing irritation.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits smoothing and relaxing effects on hair cuticles, thereby reducing frizz and making hair appear smoother and more manageable.
Caryophyllene: Has been shown to possess antioxidant properties that help protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, resulting in less frizz and a more youthful appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia may be used to create a tincture due to their potential ability to help soothe and calm the scalp, which can contribute to healthier hair.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are often used to create a tincture, as they are rich in antioxidants and may help to calm and regulate the scalp, potentially reducing frizz.

Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia might be used to create a tincture due to their potential ability to help regulate oil production in the scalp, which can contribute to healthier and less frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak potency in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Clean the harvested flowers thoroughly to remove dirt and debris with distilled water and gentle agitation.
Pack the cleaned flowers into a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid at a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the packed flowers in 80 proof vodka for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the plant material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its moisturizing and protective properties. The tea tree oil present in the tincture has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce irritation. This leads to a healthier and more hydrated scalp, which in turn helps to smooth out frizzy hair. The tincture also helps to lock in moisture, reducing frizz and flyaways, leaving hair looking smooth and silky. Regular use can lead to a more manageable and frizz-free hairstyle.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This compound helps with frizzy hair by its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the scalp and reduce irritation, thus minimizing frizz and promoting smooth hair.

Limonene: Limonene helps reduce frizz by its antiseptic and astringent properties, which protect the hair from environmental damage, reduce oil production, and keep the scalp healthy, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.

Linalool: Linalool's antifungal and antiseptic properties help control scalp infections and inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing frizz.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Barks: They are rich in essential oils, including cajeput oil, which helps to reduce frizz and add shine to hair.
Stems: They are a good source of melaleuca oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to control scalp irritation and frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves from mature plants during the early morning or late afternoon when moisture levels are lower.
Clean the leaves by gently swishing them in a bowl of water to remove dirt and debris for about 3 minutes.
Combine the cleaned leaves with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it for 2 weeks.
After 2 weeks gently shake the jar daily and then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container for about 10 minutes.
Store the resulting liquid in a dark glass bottle in a cool dry place for at least 4 weeks before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of citronella and lemongrass essential oils. These oils have natural astringent and antifungal properties that help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. The tincture also helps to lock in moisture, smoothing the hair cuticle and reducing flyaways, resulting in a smoother, more manageable hairstyle. Regular use can leave hair looking healthy and vibrant.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Geraniol: Exhibits emollient and moisturizing properties, helping to hydrate the hair and reduce frizz.
Limonene: Shows antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage that may contribute to frizz.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for frizzy hair because they contain high amounts of essential oils, particularly citronella oil, which helps to smooth and calm the hair.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make tinctures for frizzy hair because they contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and protect the hair.
Stems: The stems are used to make tinctures for frizzy hair because they contain a mixture of essential oils and minerals that help to strengthen and smooth the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine 1 pound of chopped material with 4 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures helps with frizzy hair because it contains oat kernel extract, which provides moisture and nourishment to the scalp and hair. The tannins and avenanthramides present in the tincture help to strengthen the hair follicles and reduce frizz by smoothing the hair cuticle. As a result, the hair becomes softer, smoother, and more manageable, reducing the appearance of frizz and flyaways. Regular use can lead to healthier-looking hair with a silky texture.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These compounds help to smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Ferulic acid: It has antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.
Oligosaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and adding manageability.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in nutrients and fatty acids that help to nourish and protect the hair.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to calm and soothe the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and other compounds that help to protect the hair from damage and promote healthy growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh avena sativa roots and 2 parts of fresh avena sativa leaves in late summer.
Chop the harvested avena sativa roots and leaves into small pieces to release their active compounds.
Combine 1 part of chopped avena sativa with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking.
Strain the liquid from the jar and discard the solids to obtain a herbal avena sativa tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of essential oils, which work to balance and regulate the scalp's natural pH. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture help to tighten and smooth the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the tincture's antiseptic properties help to eliminate impurities and product buildup on the hair, promoting a healthy and smooth hair texture. Regular use can lead to shinier, more manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps to reduce frizz by its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to control scalp infections and maintain a healthy scalp environment.
Caryophyllene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation on the scalp and preventing damage to hair follicles, thereby reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.

Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can damage hair and contribute to frizz, while also helping to balance the scalp's pH and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are used to make tinctures for frizzy hair because they are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that help to smooth and calm the hair.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to make tinctures for frizzy hair because they contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce frizz and add shine to the hair.
Leaves: Thyme leaves are also used to make tinctures for frizzy hair because they have antifungal properties that help to control scalp infections and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped thymus vulgaris with ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Strain the thymus vulgaris tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar.
Label and date the thymus vulgaris tincture bottles and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its natural ability to balance the scalp's pH levels and reduce inflammation. The tannins present in Equisetum arvense have astringent properties, which help to tighten the hair cuticles and smooth out the hair shaft, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing the appearance of frizzy hair. Regular use may lead to smoother, more manageable locks.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with frizzy hair by reducing surface tension and improving hair elasticity, allowing for smoother and more manageable locks.
Furostanol glycosides: Help with frizzy hair by acting as natural humectants, retaining moisture and preventing dryness that can lead to frizz and unmanageability.
Flavonoids: Help with frizzy hair by offering antioxidant properties, protecting the hair from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors that can lead to frizz and breakage.
remedy_parts: Stems: They are used due to their ability to provide hydration and structure to hair.
Roots: They are used for their potential to strengthen hair and reduce frizz.
Leaves: They are used for their possible benefits in soothing and calming the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried equisetum arvense roots and stems and store them in a clean environment.
Combine the dried equisetum arvense with 750ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container to remove solids.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata tinctures helps with frizzy hair because of its excellent hair smoothing properties. The tannins present in Cassia auriculata have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce frizz and add shine to the hair. By controlling the production of sebum and reducing inflammation in the scalp, Cassia auriculata tincture helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, leading to healthier and more manageable hair. Regular use of Cassia auriculata tincture can also improve hair texture and reduce frizz, leaving hair looking smooth and silky.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture, reducing frizz by promoting healthier hair growth.
Alkaloids: Act as a natural astringent, tightening the hair cuticles and reducing frizz by controlling moisture levels in the hair.
Gallic acid: Exhibits antioxidant properties, protecting the hair from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, which can contribute to frizz and unmanageability.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their reported properties of strengthening hair and preventing frizz.
Stems: Used due to their potential in improving hair texture and reducing frizz.
Roots: Used due to their supposed ability to nourish and smooth the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried cassia auriculata flowers and 500ml of 80% ethanol vodka at room temperature.
Steep the dried flowers in the vodka for 4-6 weeks in a dark glass container with a lid.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Label the bottle with the date and contents and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
